patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v2 0/2] syscall_user_dispatch: add build/runtime configs

series_items.subject: [PATCH v2 1/2] syscall_user_dispatch: Make it configurable in Kconfig
series_items.patch_summary: This patch makes the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable in Kconfig, allowing it to be enabled or disabled independently of CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL. The feature is used to intercept and redirect system calls outside an allowed IP address range to a userspace signal handler. By making it configurable, users can choose whether to enable this feature on their system.

review_comments.author: Matthew Wilcox
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Matthew Wilcox expressed confusion about the naming convention used in the patch, specifically that 'IP' is being used for both Internet Protocol and Instruction Pointer, which he finds unclear.
review_comments.raw_body:
I was very confused when I read this.  IP expands to Internet Protocol
long before it gets to Instruction Pointer in my brain.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch does not need to be changed because it was following a previous explanation from tglx, and thus no fix is planned.
review_comments.raw_body:
Hah, fair, i was just going with tglx's [1] explanation.

https://lore.kernel.org/all/87a4s8m69c.ffs@fw13/

review_comments.author: Thomas Gleixner
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted a space/tab mismatch in the code and pointed out that the mechanism allows filtering either outside or inside an allowed IP address range, depending on the mode passed to the PRCTL
review_comments.raw_body:
Space/TAB mismatch.

Also the mechanism allows to filter either outside an allowed range or
inside an allowed range depending on the mode which is handed to the PRCTL.

review_comments.author: Randy Dunlap
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Randy Dunlap simply echoed the original patch description, indicating a lack of specific feedback or concerns.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about inconsistent language in the patch and agreed to use more general terms.
review_comments.raw_body:
hadn't noticed that feature actually got merged.

ack, will just go with the general language

series_items.subject: [PATCH v2 2/2] syscall_user_dispatch: add kernel.syscall_user_dispatch sysctl
series_items.patch_summary: This patch series adds a sysctl to control whether userspace can arm syscall user dispatch, which allows translating non-Linux system calls into Linux system calls. The sysctl is added to the kernel and documentation, and the code is modified to respect the new sysctl setting.

review_comments.author: Thomas Gleixner
review_comments.summary: Identified an issue with include ordering in the patch, suggesting that it should be corrected to follow alphabetical order. Also pointed out that proc_dobool() exists for a reason and should not be modified.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Fri, Jul 03 2026 at 21:58, Gregory Price wrote:
> --- a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
> +++ b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
> @@ -11,12 +11,15 @@
>  #include <linux/uaccess.h>
>  #include <linux/signal.h>
>  #include <linux/elf.h>
> +#include <linux/sysctl.h>

It's already not ordered correctly, but the rule is that we fix up the
include order to alphabetic ordering when we touch it.

>  #include <linux/sched/signal.h>
>  #include <linux/sched/task_stack.h>
>  
>  #include <asm/syscall.h>
>  
> +static int syscall_user_dispatch_allowed __read_mostly = 1;
> +
>  static void trigger_sigsys(struct pt_regs *regs)
>  {
>  	struct kernel_siginfo info;
> @@ -102,6 +105,10 @@ static int task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(struct task_struct *task, unsigned lon
>  		return -EINVAL;
>  	}
>  
> +	/* Arming can be denied at runtime via sysctl, disarming is allowed */
> +	if (mode != PR_SYS_DISPATCH_OFF && !syscall_user_dispatch_allowed)
> +		return -EPERM;
> +
>  	/*
>  	 * access_ok() will clear memory tags for tagged addresses
>  	 * if current has memory tagging enabled.
> @@ -172,3 +179,24 @@ int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task, unsigned long siz
>  	return task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(task, cfg.mode, cfg.offset, cfg.len,
>  					      (char __user *)(uintptr_t)cfg.selector);
>  }
> +
> +#ifdef CONFIG_SYSCTL
> +static const struct ctl_table syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls[] = {
> +	{
> +		.procname	= "syscall_user_dispatch",
> +		.data		= &syscall_user_dispatch_allowed,
> +		.maxlen		= sizeof(syscall_user_dispatch_allowed),
> +		.mode		= 0644,
> +		.proc_handler	= proc_dointvec_minmax,

proc_dobool() exists for a reason.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price
review_comments.summary: Acknowledged Thomas Gleixner's feedback and agreed to correct the include ordering issue.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sun, Jul 05, 2026 at 10:35:08PM +0200, Thomas Gleixner wrote:
> >  #include <linux/signal.h>
> >  #include <linux/elf.h>
> > +#include <linux/sysctl.h>
> 
> It's already not ordered correctly, but the rule is that we fix up the
> include order to alphabetic ordering when we touch it.
>

ack.

> > +#ifdef CONFIG_SYSCTL
> > +static const struct ctl_table syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls[] = {
> > +	{
> > +		.procname	= "syscall_user_dispatch",
> > +		.data		= &syscall_user_dispatch_allowed,
> > +		.maxlen		= sizeof(syscall_user_dispatch_allowed),
> > +		.mode		= 0644,
> > +		.proc_handler	= proc_dointvec_minmax,
> 
> proc_dobool() exists for a reason.

ack.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds a new configuration option to make the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable at both build and runtime. The feature is currently enabled by default for all architectures, but some users may not want it functional at runtime. A new sysctl (kernel.syscall_user_dispatch) is added to control this feature, which can be disabled independently of the CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL configuration option. Disabling the feature while a task has already enabled it will not cause it to become inactive immediately, but rather only on the next attempt to re-enable via prctl or ptrace.

review_comments.author: Matthew Wilcox

review_comments.raw_body:
I was very confused when I read this.  IP expands to Internet Protocol
long before it gets to Instruction Pointer in my brain.

review_comments.summary: reviewer noted that the syscall_user_dispatch mechanism can be used to filter either within or outside of a specified range, depending on the mode passed to the prctl call, and pointed out a space/tab mismatch in the code
review_comments.raw_body:
Space/TAB mismatch.

Also the mechanism allows to filter either outside an allowed range or

It's already not ordered correctly, but the rule is that we fix up the
